codeReview.summary: The rerun does remove the exact double-load divergence called out in the prior review by threading one Session snapshot from OffscreenClient.reconcileForScoopSelection() into ChatPanel.switchToContext(), but it still does not make a clicked mid-stream assistant continue rendering. Once the original 4fa795e seeding helper and this snapshot-unification patch are combined, the selected scoop can enter a seeded-id path where OffscreenClient emits only content_delta for an already-hydrated assistant row; ChatPanel never re-adopts that row as the active stream, so the continuation text is buffered and then discarded.

issues.description: Commit 22edcf1 fixes the two-SessionStore-read race, but it leaves the seeded continuation path broken. `switchToContext()` resets `currentStreamId` and hydrates persisted messages with `isStreaming: false` (`chat-panel.ts:183-206`), while the seeded branch from the original 4fa795e fix makes `OffscreenClient.handleAgentEvent()` emit only `content_delta` for the hydrated message id (`offscreen-client.ts:422-429`) and intentionally skips `message_start`. When the next post-click delta reaches `handleContentDelta()`, it only appends to `pendingDeltaText`; `flushPendingDelta()` immediately returns because `currentStreamId` is still null (`chat-panel.ts:1060-1063`), and `handleContentDone()` later clears the pending buffer without appending it (`chat-panel.ts:738-744`). Net effect: the duplicate-row race is replaced by a silent continuation drop, so this rerun still does not fully resolve the original selection-time hydration failure.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The shared architecture notes captured the need to reuse the same SessionStore snapshot for selection hydration, but they did not also record the downstream ChatPanel requirement that a hydrated in-progress message must become the active stream before seeded `content_delta` events can flush. That omission made the 22edcf1 fix look complete even though continuation text was still dropped.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `.factory/library/architecture.md:142-149` documents persisted pre-click state plus snapshot-consistent hydration, but does not mention `ChatPanel.currentStreamId` / `flushPendingDelta`. The worker's own handoff for `fix-unify-selection-session-read` also records the missing downstream behavior in `handoff.discoveredIssues[0]` of `handoffs/2026-04-18T06-30-44-999Z__fix-unify-selection-session-read__05bc1db3-e1e3-4e07-b4b3-3c1f77a891e5.json`.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The feature spec mixed two different race variants, which weakened the regression test. It asked the user-only S1 race to preserve hydrated `msg-5`, but the committed test only asserted 'one assistant row' and explicitly accepted a synthesized `scoop-<jid>-<uid>` row. That ambiguity left room for a green test suite that still missed the real mid-stream continuation failure.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `features.json:1862-1883` step 8 requires the user-only S1 race test to end with one assistant row whose id is `msg-5`; the committed regression in the reviewed commit (`packages/webapp/tests/ui/selection-session-race.test.ts:186-196`) only asserts row count and comments that the post-fix row is sourced from the delta's synthesized id. The worker echoed the mismatch in `handoff.skillFeedback.suggestedChanges[0]` of `handoffs/2026-04-18T06-30-44-999Z__fix-unify-selection-session-read__05bc1db3-e1e3-4e07-b4b3-3c1f77a891e5.json`.

summary: Fail. Commit 22edcf1 correctly unifies the reconcile and hydration SessionStore reads that caused the prior scrutiny failure, but it still leaves the seeded-message continuation path broken: once a mid-stream assistant row is hydrated, subsequent `text_delta` events are buffered and dropped because ChatPanel has no active stream id for that row. The rerun therefore does not adequately address the original blocking issue.
